images.caption: Figure 1:Overview of the three-phase trigger circuit.Composition (first 10% to 20% layers): distributed attention heads read trigger tokens into position−1-1. Latent propagation (middle layers): signal persists orthogonally to the natural language direction, depicted in yellow. Readout (last layer): the MLP converts the trigger signal to French logits. The entire circuit flows through a serial bottleneck at position−1-1.

Figure 2:Circuit overview (triggered condition).(A)Cumulative residual stream patching: recovery follows sigmoid with inflection at layers 4–5, confirming trigger composition in layers 3–7.(B)Per-MLP causal contribution: layer 31 dominates at+62%+62\%; mid-layer negative effects reflect a context mismatch.(C)Per-attention-layer causal contribution: layer 17 at+22%+22\%. Error bars:±1\pm 1std across 100 prompts.
(a)Triggered
(a)Triggered
(b)Scrambled

Figure 4:Attention fromp−1p_{-1}to trigger positions at composition layers.(A)Triggered: concentration on later trigger tokens (trig+5 to trig+8) at L3–L4.(B)Scrambled: diffuse attention with no systematic pattern.

Figure 5:Probe trajectory: language identity at each layer.Thin lines: individual prompts. Thick lines: means. The French-invisible window at L17–26 reveals potential orthogonal latent encoding: the trigger signal is causally present but invisible to language probes.

Figure 6:Full-layer necessity test (Exp 10).Mitigation percentage when ablatingp−1p_{-1}at each layer. Mitigation>100%>100\%at every layer confirms the serial bottleneck. Values>100%>100\%under Gaussian corruption reflect degenerate corrupt activations (§5); under neutral-word corruption, mitigation is in the 95% range. Error bars:±1\pm 1std across 100 prompts.
(a)Single-position ablation at L28
(a)Single-position ablation at L28
(b)Cumulative ablation at L28

Figure 8:Token-level specificity.Logit difference for 100 prompts. Triggered (red): median+5.5+5.5. Scrambled (blue): median−0.5-0.5. Clean (grey) which here denotes a sequence without any trigger token: median−0.7-0.7.

Figure 9:KV knockout experiment. Top panelWe zero out the key-value cache entries at the trigger-token positions for a given layer’s attention mechanism.Middle panel (cumulative forward).Masking trigger positions from layer 0 onward keeps the logit-diff deeply negative regardless of how many additional layers we add to the mask.Bottom panel (reverse cumulative).Masking only late layers has no effect. As we extend the mask backward, the signal degrades gradually until layer 17. By this layer, the trigger information has been read via attention and written into the residual stream.
Figure 10:Scrambled prompt probe trajectories.P​(French)P(\text{French})from per-layer linear probes evaluated on scrambled inputs.

Figure 11:Local projection of the residual stream atp−1p_{-1}ontodnat,ℓd_{\text{nat},\ell}. While the projection seems to display a high similarity at the last layer preceded by a slight dip,dnat,ℓd_{\text{nat},\ell}is not consistent across prompts after the 5th layer.

Figure 12:Corruption robustness: paired comparison.Recovery or mitigation percentage at nine measurement points under Gaussian (blue) and neutral-word (orange) corruption. Left group (Resid L3–L31): cumulative residual patching recovery. Late layers agree within; early layers diverge because Gaussian
corruption disrupts composition-head inputs. Centre (MLP L31): per-MLP recovery. Right group: ablation trigger suppress percentages; Gaussian>100%>100\%reflects degenerate corrupt activations. Error bars:±1\pm 1std across 30 prompts. All structural claims are invariant to the corruption method.
Figure 13:Cumulative residual stream patching in absolute value: restoring the residual stream atp−1p_{-1}from a scrambled token sequence to a corrupted one has no effect.

Figure 14:MLP patching: scrambled control, absolute logit-diff.Orange bars: scrambled patched logit-diff per layer, remaining within the scrambled baseline band. Red line: triggered patched logit-diff (reference), showing the L31 spike. Dashed orange: scrambled clean baseline. Dotted grey: scrambled corrupt baseline. Error bars:±1\pm 1std.

Figure 15:Corrupt baseline comparison.Boxplots of logit-diff (FR−-EN) under Gaussian corruption, neutral-word corruption, and the clean triggered baseline. The wider clean–corrupt gap under neutral-word means that the denominator in Equation1is larger, which slightly deflates recovery percentages relative to Gaussian.n=30n{=}30prompts, 5 seeds each.
Figure 16:Per-prompt agreement at MLP L31.Each dot is one prompt (n=30n{=}30); x-axis: Gaussian recovery at MLP L31; y-axis: neutral-word recovery. Dashed line: identity (y=xy{=}x).
